signal_title: Children and Teens’ Online Privacy Protection Act (H.R.7890)
risk_rationale: The new legislation strengthens protections for minors' online privacy, which may introduce stricter requirements than the current baseline, particularly in data collection and usage for minors under 18.

compliance_gaps.baseline_requirement: TikTok states personalized ads are not allowed for under-18 users.
compliance_gaps.regulatory_requirement: Strengthens protections related to the online collection, use, and disclosure of personal information of children and teens.
compliance_gaps.gap_description: The new act may require additional privacy safeguards beyond the current restriction on personalized ads, potentially affecting how data is collected and used for minors.

compliance_gaps.baseline_requirement: TikTok Privacy Policy (non-US / global page) last updated 2025-07-08.
compliance_gaps.regulatory_requirement: Amends COPPA to enhance privacy safeguards for minors.
compliance_gaps.gap_description: The amendment to COPPA may necessitate updates to the privacy policy to reflect enhanced safeguards for minors.

remediations.title: Update Privacy Policy for Minors
remediations.description: Revise the privacy policy to incorporate enhanced safeguards for minors as per the new legislation.

remediations.title: Enhance Data Collection Practices
remediations.description: Implement additional data protection measures for minors, ensuring compliance with the strengthened privacy requirements.

business_impact: Potential increase in compliance costs and operational changes to align with new privacy requirements for minors.
recommended_actions: Conduct a legal review of the new legislation
Communicate changes to users and stakeholders
